mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A 0.5 percentage point drop in FEDFUNDS over 3 months signals monetary easing and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.4% as of Jan 2005 reflects post-2004 rate hikes
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns in periods following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, which drives corporate earnings expectations upward and supports equity valuations over a 5-trading-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151,431k as of Jan 2005 reflects strong labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer demand and near-term earnings growth. When RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month, equity valuations expand over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations upward.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns in high-growth RSAFS months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. Declining INDPRO signals demand weakness and margin compression, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: early-stage demand slowdown or supply shock
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following consecutive INDPRO declines are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate simultaneously, equity valuations expand as investors price in sustained consumer-driven earnings growth over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.4%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,253k and RSAFS at 523,514M as of Jan 2005
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when both conditions are met are not significantly higher than when only one is met (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. When UNRATE rises above 4.0%, equity valuations contract over 60 trading days as investors reprice earnings downward due to expected consumer spending weakness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% as of Jan 2005 remains below historical average
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE increases are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation pressure (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary tightening that constrains equity valuations. When both conditions hold, S&P 500 returns are negative over 20 trading days as investors price in higher discount rates and margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 256.16 and FEDFUNDS at 2.4% as of Jan 2005
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when both conditions are met are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic activity. Quarterly GDPC1 growth above 3.0% annualized signals robust demand and earnings expansion, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors maintain elevated equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 3.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
